steps.title: Identify the right triangle dimensions
steps.content: The figure shows a right triangle with a right angle marked at the bottom-left corner. The two legs of the triangle (the sides that form the right angle) are labeled: vertical leg = 3 inches, horizontal leg = 5 inches.

steps.title: Apply the right triangle area formula
steps.content:
For a right triangle, the area equals half the product of the two legs:

$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times \text{leg}_1 \times \text{leg}_2$$

$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 3 = \frac{15}{2} = 7.5$$

The area is 7.5 square inches.
